denominator_used: N = 1 pathway row (HEDGEHOG_SIGNALING) within the Top 10 Pathways block from CpG island hypermethylation in MPNST-G1, from sheet 'Fig 1f+g' of 41467_2023_38432_MOESM10_ESM.xlsx
assumptions_made: The single Top 10 Pathways block in sheet 'Fig 1f+g' corresponds to CpG island hypermethylation in MPNST-G1 as indicated by the data context; the numeric column labeled '-log(FDR corrected P-value)' in row 3 of the sheet is the requested value; HEDGEHOG_SIGNALING appears as the first pathway in the list.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM10_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Fig 1f+g'
   - Shape: 13 rows x 2 columns
   - Relevant columns: 'Pathway' (column A) and '-log(FDR corrected P-value)' (column B)

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Located the Top 10 Pathways table block (header row 1 = 'Top 10 Pathways'; column headers row 3: Pathway / -log(FDR corrected P-value))
   - Data rows 4-13 contain 10 pathways for the MPNST-G1 CpG island hypermethylation analysis
   - Filter: row where Pathway == 'HEDGEHOG_SIGNALING' -> 1 row (row 4)

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Outcome variable: '-log(FDR corrected P-value)' numeric value in column B
   - Pathway identifier: string value in column A matched exactly to 'HEDGEHOG_SIGNALING'

D. COMPUTATION
   - Read the worksheet using pandas (openpyxl engine) with header=None to preserve layout
   - Verified via openpyxl cell inspection: cell A4 = 'HEDGEHOG_SIGNALING', cell B4 = 3.645892
   - No merged cells; no secondary block present in this sheet; the only Top 10 Pathways block is present

E. RESULT
   - -log(FDR corrected P-value) for HEDGEHOG_SIGNALING = 3.645892
   - This tied with ESTROGEN_EARLY_RESPONSE for the top rank in the list.

F. CAVEATS
   - The sheet 'Fig 1f+g' contains only one Top 10 Pathways block without an explicit in-cell label identifying it as MPNST-G1 vs MPNST-G2; reliance on the data context for attribution to MPNST-G1.
   - The value is read directly from the deposited source data and reported as given (no recalculation of FDR was performed).
